# agent-policy

Vendor-neutral CLI to keep AI coding-agent instruction files in sync and enforce commit attribution.

## Project overview

`agent-policy` is a small TypeScript/Node CLI and a companion Claude
Code plugin (`47nation`). It reads one canonical `agent-policy.yaml`
and generates `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and
`.github/copilot-instructions.md` deterministically. A `commit-msg`
hook enforces an `Assisted-by:` attribution footer.

This repo self-hosts: the file you are reading was rendered from
`agent-policy.yaml` at the repo root by `agent-policy sync`.

## Stack and layout

- Node 20+, TypeScript strict, ESM.
- CLI: `commander`. YAML: `yaml`. Schema: `ajv` (draft 2020-12).
- Tests: `vitest`. Build: `tsup` bundles to `dist/cli.js`.
- `src/config/` — YAML loader, Ajv validator, typed model.
- `src/renderers/` — pure renderers (`agents-md`, `claude-md`,
  `copilot`) plus `common.ts` (banner+hash) and `compose.ts`
  (section filter + attribution composer).
- `src/commands/` — one file per CLI subcommand.
- `src/io/` — filesystem with banner guard, git hooks-dir
  resolution, commit-trailer extractor.
- `schemas/agent-policy.schema.json` — canonical JSON Schema.
- `integrations/claude-code/47nation/` — Claude Code plugin.

## Conventions

- Pure core, I/O at the edges. `config/` and `renderers/` do not
  touch the filesystem; `commands/` and `io/` do.
- Small, cohesive modules. No circular imports — renderer helpers
  live in `renderers/compose.ts`, not in `index.ts`.
- No comments that restate what the code does. Only keep comments
  that capture non-obvious *why*.
- Tests must not write to the working directory. Use
  `mkdtempSync(tmpdir(), ...)`. Tests that exercise git initialize
  their own repo with `execSync('git init -q', { cwd: tmpDir })`.
- Determinism: same config → same bytes. Snapshot and byte-equality
  tests exist; do not break them casually.

## Adding a new renderer

Full step-by-step in `docs/renderers.md`. Short version:
1. Add the id to the `RendererId` union in `src/config/types.ts`.
2. Add it to the JSON Schema's `$defs/section/properties/renderers/items/enum`
   and to `properties/renderers/properties/<id>` pointing at `$defs/renderer`.
3. Implement the renderer in `src/renderers/<id>.ts`, importing
   `wrapWithBanner` from `common.ts` and `filterSections` /
   `composeAttributionSection` from `compose.ts`.
4. Register it in `src/renderers/index.ts`.
5. Add a snapshot test under `tests/`.
The registry-driven `sync` and `sync --check` pick it up automatically.

## Running tests

- `npm test` runs all 68 tests on vitest 4.
- `npm run typecheck` — strict tsc with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`.
- `npm run lint` — eslint.
- `npm run build` — tsup bundle.
- End-to-end smoke: `(cd examples/full && node ../../dist/cli.js sync --check)`.

## Commit attribution

Every commit in this repo must end with an `Assisted-by:` footer.
- AI-assisted: `Assisted-by: {agent}:{model}` (e.g.
  `Assisted-by: claude:opus-4-7`). Multiple footers allowed
  for multi-agent commits.
- Human-only: `Assisted-by: n/a`.
The `commit-msg` hook enforces this. Install with
`agent-policy install-hooks` after cloning.

## Release flow

See `docs/publishing.md`. In short:
1. Update `CHANGELOG.md` (`[Unreleased]` → new version).
2. `npm version patch|minor|major` to bump + tag.
3. `git push origin main && git push origin <tag>`.
4. The release workflow (currently disabled pending NPM_TOKEN)
   publishes to npm with provenance.

## Attribution

All commits must end with an `Assisted-by:` footer.

- AI-assisted: `Assisted-by: {agent}:{model}`
- Human-only: `Assisted-by: n/a`
- Multiple footers are allowed for multi-vendor work.
